title: Unified system of record
requirement: The platform shall maintain a governed, authoritative system of record linking strategy, mission, core functions, demands, approved projects, resources, costs, risks, decisions, outcomes, and realized benefits across DDC5I.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Strategy hierarchy
requirement: The platform shall represent configurable strategic hierarchies including Joint Staff priorities, DDC5I priorities, directorate objectives, division objectives, mission outcomes, lines of effort, and measurable key results.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Mission and function catalog
requirement: The platform shall maintain a version-controlled catalog of DDC5I missions, statutory or directed functions, recurring core functions, products, services, customers, supported commands, and Joint or coalition effects.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Bidirectional traceability
requirement: Every demand, core function, investment, and project shall trace to at least one mission, objective, or approved mandate, and leaders shall be able to navigate from strategy to execution and from execution back to strategy.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Unaligned work control
requirement: The platform shall identify work lacking mission alignment, record the justification or exception authority, and prevent approval unless the required waiver is documented.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Governance calendar
requirement: The platform shall schedule and track recurring portfolio governance events, submission cutoffs, decision forums, budget drills, quarterly reviews, and annual planning cycles.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Decision rights
requirement: The platform shall encode configurable decision authorities, thresholds, quorum rules, delegation, and escalation paths by demand type, value, risk, division, and funding level.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Decision record
requirement: Each governance decision shall capture the decision, date, authority, participants, rationale, conditions, dissent or caveats, affected items, and required follow-up actions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio policies
requirement: Administrators shall manage versioned business rules for intake, scoring, stage gates, status reporting, resource assignment, financial control, change approval, and closure.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Planning horizons
requirement: The platform shall support current execution, budget year, POM or multi-year planning, and user-defined planning horizons without losing historical baselines.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Enterprise and division roll-up
requirement: The platform shall aggregate data from JAD, DSD, AID, CID, JFID, and C3OD2 into a DDC5I enterprise portfolio while preserving division ownership and visibility boundaries.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio taxonomy
requirement: The platform shall apply a common configurable taxonomy for demand type, mission area, capability, customer, priority, lifecycle stage, project health, resource type, skill, cost category, and benefit type.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Operating model flexibility
requirement: The platform shall support waterfall, agile, hybrid, recurring operations, level-of-effort work, rapid-response taskers, and non-project core functions within one portfolio model.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Governance evidence
requirement: The platform shall retain the evidence package used for each approval or prioritization decision, including attachments, scores, comments, resource estimates, and financial assumptions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Outcome accountability
requirement: Approved investments shall identify an accountable outcome owner, expected end state, measurable benefits, review dates, and the decision that will be informed by benefit results.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Organization hierarchy
requirement: The platform shall represent DDC5I, its six division portfolios, subordinate branches or teams, matrixed project teams, external organizations, and configurable future organizational changes.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Role-based workspaces
requirement: The platform shall provide tailored workspaces for senior leaders, division chiefs, branch chiefs, portfolio managers, demand managers, project managers, resource managers, financial managers, team members, approvers, data stewards, auditors, and administrators.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Named accountability
requirement: Every active demand, project, core function, risk, decision, benefit, resource plan, and financial plan shall have a named accountable owner and an alternate where required.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Lead and support relationships
requirement: The platform shall record lead, supporting, consulted, informed, approving, and beneficiary relationships for internal and external stakeholders at portfolio, project, milestone, and deliverable levels.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: External stakeholder register
requirement: The platform shall maintain an external stakeholder register with organization, mission relationship, role, expected contribution, dependency, commitment, point of contact, and engagement status.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: RACI matrices
requirement: Users shall generate and maintain RACI or equivalent responsibility matrices from stored roles and assignments, with conflict detection for missing or duplicate accountability.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Delegation and acting roles
requirement: Authorized users shall establish time-bounded delegations and acting roles for approval, reporting, and project responsibilities, with full audit history.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Conflict-of-interest control
requirement: The platform shall permit designated reviewers to record conflicts of interest and recuse or reassign assessors without deleting the audit trail.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Contact and directory synchronization
requirement: The platform shall synchronize approved identity, organization, title, and contact attributes from the authoritative directory while preventing unauthorized local overwrite.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Stakeholder contribution reporting
requirement: Leaders shall view planned and actual contributions by internal and external stakeholder, including labor, deliverables, funding, decisions, and dependencies.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Division-specific configuration
requirement: Each division shall be able to maintain authorized local views, intake forms, scoring questions, reporting schedules, and reference data while inheriting mandatory DDC5I enterprise controls.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Organizational change history
requirement: The platform shall preserve historical ownership and reporting relationships when organizations, roles, or projects are realigned.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Single intake portal
requirement: The platform shall provide a common, accessible intake portal for ideas, demands, taskers, modernization initiatives, mandates, unfunded requirements, operational needs, and change requests.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Guided intake
requirement: The intake experience shall use conditional questions, help text, examples, required fields, and progressive disclosure based on demand type and requester role.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Minimum intake data
requirement: Every submitted demand shall capture requester, sponsoring organization, title, problem or opportunity, mission need, desired outcome, required date, beneficiaries, urgency, consequences of inaction, preliminary scope, and known dependencies.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Purpose and end state
requirement: The intake shall require a clear purpose statement, measurable end state, and explanation of value to the Joint Staff and Joint or coalition forces before assessment begins.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Demand categories
requirement: Authorized administrators shall configure demand categories including named project, recurring core function, emergent tasker, compliance mandate, capability gap, technical debt, process improvement, data or standards work, and innovation experiment.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Core-function demand
requirement: Users shall record workload demands against recurring core functions even when the work does not qualify as a formal project.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Attachment and evidence intake
requirement: Requesters shall attach or link supporting documents, emails, directives, estimates, presentations, data, and prior decisions, subject to security and file controls.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Draft and collaborate
requirement: Requesters shall save drafts, invite co-authors, track revisions, and submit only when validation rules are satisfied.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Duplicate detection
requirement: The platform shall identify potentially duplicate or overlapping demands using configurable metadata and text similarity and allow reviewers to merge, link, or dismiss the match.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Demand relationships
requirement: Users shall link demands as duplicates, predecessors, successors, alternatives, parent-child items, bundled requests, or mutually exclusive options.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Intake SLA
requirement: The platform shall measure acknowledgment, triage, assessment, decision, and requester-notification times against configurable service-level targets.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Return for clarification
requirement: Reviewers shall return a demand for clarification with specific questions, due dates, status, and an auditable response thread.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Sensitive submission
requirement: Authorized requesters shall mark and route sensitive demands so that metadata and attachments are visible only to approved roles.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Bulk intake
requirement: Authorized users shall create or update demands through a validated Excel template and receive row-level success and error results.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Email-originated demand
requirement: Authorized users shall convert an Outlook email or approved shared-mailbox message into a draft demand while preserving the source message reference and attachments allowed by policy.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Demand status transparency
requirement: Requesters shall see the current stage, owner, next action, target decision date, pending information, and final disposition of their submissions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Demand aging and backlog
requirement: The platform shall report demand aging, stalled items, backlog by division and category, and demands approaching or exceeding decision SLAs.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Withdraw and resubmit
requirement: Requesters shall withdraw eligible demands and create a new revision from a declined, deferred, or closed demand without altering the original record.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Configurable assessment
requirement: The platform shall route each demand through a configurable assessment appropriate to its type, value, urgency, sensitivity, and anticipated resource impact.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Mission-alignment score
requirement: The assessment shall measure alignment to DDC5I mission, Joint Staff priorities, division objectives, directed mandates, and Joint or coalition operational outcomes.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Value and ROI assessment
requirement: The assessment shall capture quantitative and qualitative benefits, cost avoidance, readiness or interoperability impact, risk reduction, time saved, customer reach, and confidence in each estimate.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Resource feasibility
requirement: The assessment shall compare preliminary people, skill, time, funding, infrastructure, contracting, and external-support requirements against forecast availability.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Risk assessment
requirement: The assessment shall capture delivery, operational, technical, security, acquisition, financial, schedule, dependency, and opportunity risks using configurable likelihood and consequence scales.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Urgency and consequence
requirement: The platform shall distinguish urgency from importance and record operational, statutory, fiscal, or reputational consequences if work is delayed or not performed.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Scoring model
requirement: Authorized governance users shall configure weighted scoring models, mandatory gates, tie-breakers, normalization rules, and score bands without source-code changes.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Recommended scoring baseline
requirement: The initial model shall support mission criticality, strategic alignment, operational impact, urgency, risk reduction, readiness or interoperability contribution, feasibility, and expected value, with weights approved during design.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Mandatory demand override
requirement: Directed, statutory, safety, cybersecurity, and time-critical demands shall be identifiable as mandatory and shall retain an explanation when ranked outside the normal score order.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Independent scoring
requirement: The platform shall support multiple assessors, blind or independent scoring where required, aggregation, variance detection, comments, and adjudication.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio comparison
requirement: Reviewers shall compare candidate demands side by side by alignment, value, risk, cost, resource demand, readiness, dependencies, and confidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Options analysis
requirement: Reviewers shall record alternatives including approve, decline, defer, combine, pilot, re-scope, perform as core work, outsource, or request additional analysis.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: What must stop
requirement: When approving work beyond available capacity, the decision package shall identify work to stop, delay, reduce, or de-scope, or shall document an approved capacity increase.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Approval workflow
requirement: The platform shall route approvals sequentially or in parallel based on configured authorities and shall support approve, reject, request changes, abstain, delegate, and approve with conditions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Decision package
requirement: The platform shall generate a decision-ready package containing the demand summary, alignment, score, alternatives, resource and cost estimates, risks, dependencies, stakeholder views, and staff recommendation.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Decision conditions
requirement: Approved conditions shall become trackable actions, constraints, milestones, or controls on the resulting project or work item.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Deferred review
requirement: Deferred demands shall carry a reason, reconsideration trigger or date, owner, and refreshed resource or mission assumptions at the next review.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Conversion to execution
requirement: An approved demand shall create or link to a project, program, recurring work package, or portfolio epic without rekeying approved data and shall preserve demand-to-delivery traceability.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio hierarchy
requirement: The platform shall support the DDC5I enterprise portfolio, six division portfolios, subordinate portfolios, programs, projects, products, services, and core-function work packages.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio inventory
requirement: Leaders shall view all proposed, approved, active, on-hold, completed, canceled, and archived work, including non-project core functions and unfunded requirements.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Named and core work distinction
requirement: The platform shall distinguish named projects from recurring core functions while enabling both to compete for and report the same constrained resources.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Roadmaps
requirement: Users shall create time-phased roadmaps by enterprise, division, mission, capability, customer, product, program, and strategic objective.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio health
requirement: The platform shall calculate transparent portfolio health using configurable schedule, cost, scope, resource, risk, dependency, benefit, data-quality, milestone, and reporting-currency thresholds.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Health override
requirement: Authorized owners may override calculated health only with rationale, duration, and an audit record showing the calculated and reported states.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Interdependency map
requirement: Users shall visualize cross-division and external dependencies among missions, core functions, projects, milestones, resources, systems, data products, standards, decisions, and stakeholders.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Critical path across projects
requirement: The platform shall identify portfolio-level milestones and dependencies whose delay would affect multiple projects, divisions, or mission outcomes.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Roadblock management
requirement: Users shall record roadblocks with owner, severity, impacted work, decision needed, target resolution, escalation level, aging, and resolution evidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Strategic balance
requirement: The platform shall show investment balance across mission areas, divisions, demand types, time horizons, risk levels, beneficiaries, and modernization versus sustainment.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio baseline
requirement: Authorized users shall create time-stamped portfolio baselines covering scope, priority, schedule, resources, costs, benefits, and assumptions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Change history
requirement: The platform shall show what changed from each portfolio baseline, who authorized the change, and the resulting impacts.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Unfunded requirement management
requirement: The platform shall maintain an unfunded requirements list with priority, minimum viable funding, full requirement, consequence, decision window, and sponsor.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Stop-start-continue analysis
requirement: Leaders shall identify work to start, stop, continue, accelerate, defer, or merge and see the forecast resource and mission consequences before approval.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio review workspace
requirement: A portfolio review workspace shall combine decisions due, changes since last review, health exceptions, resource gaps, financial variances, benefits at risk, and required leader actions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Cross-division collaboration
requirement: A project may have one lead division and multiple supporting divisions, with effort, costs, deliverables, risks, and contributions attributable to each.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Archive and lessons
requirement: Closed or canceled portfolio items shall remain searchable with outcomes, lessons, reusable artifacts, final cost, resource use, and disposition of unresolved actions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Enterprise hierarchy navigation
requirement: Authorized users shall browse and drill through configurable Objective-to-Portfolio-to-Program-to-Project-to-Workstream-to-Task relationships while preserving alternate product, service, capability, and core-function structures.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Integrated portfolio schedule
requirement: The platform shall provide hierarchical schedule and Gantt views across portfolios, programs, projects, workstreams, milestones, and tasks, with filters for organization, owner, lifecycle, status, priority, and date range.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Portfolio risk analytics
requirement: Authorized users shall view configurable risk matrices, issue and risk aging, exposure trends, mitigation status, and aggregated risk by project, program, division, mission, and enterprise portfolio.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

requirement: Each approved project shall have a user-level workspace for scope, objectives, end state, team, plan, tasks, schedule, resources, costs, risks, issues, dependencies, decisions, documents, status, and benefits.

requirement: Authorized users shall create reusable templates for common DDC5I project types with default phases, milestones, tasks, checklists, roles, artifacts, gates, risks, and reporting cadence.

title: Timeline and Gantt
requirement: Users shall view and edit project schedules in timeline or Gantt views showing dependencies, milestones, baselines, critical path, and variance.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Agile delivery
requirement: Projects shall support backlogs, epics, features, stories, sprints, estimates, acceptance criteria, velocity, and retrospective notes where agile delivery is selected.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Task detail
requirement: Each task shall support owner, contributors, status, priority, dates, estimated and actual effort, percent complete, dependencies, checklist, notes, attachments, comments, tags, and acceptance evidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Dependency enforcement
requirement: The platform shall support finish-to-start, start-to-start, finish-to-finish, and external milestone dependencies and shall warn users of invalid or broken schedule relationships.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Milestone governance
requirement: Projects shall identify key decision, delivery, integration, test, policy, funding, and external milestones with criteria, owner, forecast date, confidence, and status.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: RAID management
requirement: Projects shall maintain linked risks, assumptions, issues, decisions, and dependencies with owners, aging, response plans, escalation, and closure evidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Action tracking
requirement: Meeting and governance actions shall be assigned, due-dated, linked to affected items, reminded, escalated, and closed with evidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Document management
requirement: Users shall upload, link, version, categorize, preview, and search authorized project documents and associate them with tasks, milestones, risks, decisions, or deliverables.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Status reporting
requirement: Project managers shall submit a periodic status update recording the reporting period, reporter, accomplishments, planned activities, health, variances, decisions needed, resource needs, risks, roadblocks, changes since the prior report, and supporting evidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Automatic roll-up
requirement: Approved project status, milestones, forecast dates, resource actuals, financial actuals, risks, and benefits shall feed division and senior dashboards without manual re-entry.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Baseline and change control
requirement: The platform shall baseline approved scope, schedule, cost, and resources and require authorized change control when configured tolerances are exceeded.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Deliverable acceptance
requirement: Each deliverable shall have an accountable producer, accepting authority, due date, acceptance criteria, version, disposition, and acceptance evidence.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Time and effort capture
requirement: Team members shall record or import actual effort against authorized tasks or core-function work, with approval and correction workflows appropriate to policy.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Project calendar
requirement: Project milestones, reviews, due dates, and selected tasks shall appear in a project calendar and optionally synchronize with authorized Outlook calendars.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Project closure
requirement: Closure shall verify deliverable acceptance, financial reconciliation, resource release, records disposition, lessons learned, unresolved actions, benefit ownership, and transition to operations.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Mobile and tablet execution
requirement: Authorized users shall review status, update tasks, approve actions, and access essential project information on supported tablets and mobile browsers.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: ProjectOS interoperability
requirement: The enterprise portfolio platform and ProjectOS shall use documented APIs and canonical identifiers so projects can be created, synchronized, reconciled, and rolled up without duplicate ownership of authoritative fields.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Schedule-state comparison
requirement: Project and program schedules shall distinguish approved baseline, current plan, actual dates, and forecast dates and shall calculate variance at task, milestone, workstream, project, and program levels.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Resource catalog
requirement: The platform shall maintain authorized resource records for government civilians, military personnel, contractors, shared services, teams, positions, vacancies, and non-labor resources needed for planning.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Privacy-separated workforce data
requirement: The planning model shall use only the minimum workforce attributes needed for portfolio decisions and shall separate restricted personnel data from broadly visible capacity information.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Capacity calendars
requirement: Resource capacity shall account for work schedules, leave assumptions, holidays, training, administrative overhead, duty restrictions, mobilizations, vacancies, and configurable productive-capacity factors.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Allocation by time period
requirement: Managers shall allocate resources to projects and core functions by person, role, team, skill, percentage, hours, or FTE across weeks, months, quarters, and fiscal years.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Planned versus actual effort
requirement: The platform shall compare planned allocation, approved capacity, committed effort, and actual effort by person, role, team, division, project, core function, and time period.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Over-allocation detection
requirement: The platform shall identify over-allocation, double booking, insufficient coverage, and underutilization using configurable thresholds and notify responsible managers.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Role-based planning
requirement: Planning shall be possible using generic roles or teams before named personnel are selected, with later fulfillment tracking.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Skill taxonomy
requirement: The platform shall maintain a governed taxonomy of skills, competencies, specialties, certifications, clearances or access prerequisites, proficiency levels, and currency dates.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Skill profiles
requirement: Authorized users shall maintain validated skill profiles for personnel and teams, including proficiency, evidence source, last validated date, and development interests where permitted.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Skill demand
requirement: Projects and core functions shall specify required roles, skills, proficiency, quantity, duration, location, access, and desired start date.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Skill-gap analysis
requirement: The platform shall compare current and forecast skill supply to approved and prospective demand by division and enterprise and show size, timing, and mission impact of gaps.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Resource request workflow
requirement: Project managers shall request named or role-based resources, and resource managers shall propose, approve, partially fulfill, decline, substitute, or schedule fulfillment.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Commitment confidence
requirement: Resource commitments shall include status and confidence, distinguishing requested, soft-booked, committed, assigned, onboarded, and released resources.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Vacancy and hiring impact
requirement: The platform shall represent vacancies, anticipated losses, hiring or contract actions, expected onboarding dates, and the portfolio impact if dates change.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Contingent and contractor resources
requirement: Planning shall distinguish government, military, contractor, and external partner resources and capture contract period, ceiling or capacity, labor category, and funding constraints as authorized.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Critical-role succession
requirement: Leaders shall identify single points of failure, critical roles without backups, and projects or functions at risk from anticipated personnel changes.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Training and development demand
requirement: Approved skill gaps may generate linked training, hiring, contracting, detail, or partnership actions with cost, owner, and expected readiness date.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Resource optimization
requirement: The platform shall recommend feasible resource reassignments or schedule changes based on priorities, skills, availability, constraints, and minimum staffing rules, subject to human approval.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Minimum core-function coverage
requirement: Division leaders shall define minimum staffing and critical-skill coverage for core functions, and the platform shall warn when project assignments would violate those thresholds.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: External contribution
requirement: The resource plan shall capture expected and actual labor or capability contributed by external stakeholders without treating external personnel as DDC5I-owned capacity.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Planned and unplanned demand
requirement: Capacity analysis shall include approved projects, recurring core functions, planned administrative work, forecast demand, and recorded unplanned or emergent work so available capacity is not overstated.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
title: Interactive reallocation impact
requirement: Resource managers shall model or propose reallocations and immediately view the resulting schedule, cost, skill-coverage, core-function, priority, and risk impacts before committing the change.
capability: People, Capacity, Skills, and Role Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Financial hierarchy
requirement: The platform shall support fiscal years, appropriations or funding types, programs, cost centers, lines of effort, projects, core functions, and configurable financial reference structures.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Budget lifecycle
requirement: Users shall capture requested, planned, approved, distributed, committed, obligated, expended, forecast, and remaining amounts as available from authoritative sources.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Budget versus actual
requirement: Leaders shall compare budget, commitments, obligations, expenditures, accruals where used, estimate to complete, and estimate at completion by project, function, division, portfolio, and fiscal period.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Cost categories
requirement: Costs shall be categorized at minimum as labor, contract support, travel, training, software, hardware, cloud or hosting, facilities, data, integration, and other configurable categories.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Labor cost planning
requirement: Authorized planners shall estimate labor cost from hours or FTE, role or labor category, time period, and controlled rates, while limiting visibility of sensitive rate data.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Multi-year cost profile
requirement: Projects and core functions shall maintain a multi-year cost profile with assumptions, confidence, minimum viable funding, and full-funding requirement.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Financial baseline and variance
requirement: The platform shall baseline approved financial plans and calculate period and cumulative variance with configurable thresholds, explanations, and corrective actions.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Funding constraints
requirement: Users shall record time limits, purpose restrictions, color-of-money constraints, contract dependencies, expiration risks, and other funding constraints relevant to execution.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Unfunded and underfunded status
requirement: The platform shall identify unfunded, partially funded, fully funded, overfunded, and funding-at-risk work and quantify the mission or schedule consequence.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Cost change request
requirement: A change that exceeds configured cost tolerance shall generate a financial change request showing cause, alternatives, offsets, impacts, and approval authority.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Benefit register
requirement: Investments shall maintain expected financial and nonfinancial benefits, measurement method, baseline, target, owner, realization date, confidence, and actual result.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: ROI and value framework
requirement: The platform shall calculate configurable return measures such as cost avoidance, time saved, readiness gain, risk reduction, interoperability gain, service improvement, and qualitative strategic value without forcing all value into dollars.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Financial source reconciliation
requirement: Imported or integrated financial data shall be reconciled to the authoritative source, with unmatched records, timing differences, and manual adjustments visible and auditable.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Resource-cost linkage
requirement: Approved staffing and non-labor resource plans shall roll into the cost forecast, and authorized cost changes shall show corresponding resource or scope effects.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
title: Financial closeout
requirement: Project closure shall require final cost reconciliation, disposition of remaining funds or commitments, and documentation of financial lessons or unresolved transactions.
capability: Budget, Cost, Actuals, and Investment Management
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Portfolio forecast
requirement: The platform shall forecast workload, capacity, skill gaps, schedule, cost, funding, benefits, and mission impacts for approved and candidate work across configurable time horizons.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Baseline scenario
requirement: Forecasts shall reference an approved baseline scenario so leaders can distinguish current plan, actual performance, and proposed change.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: What-if scenarios
requirement: Authorized users shall create non-destructive what-if scenarios by changing priorities, start dates, scope, staffing, skills, funding, costs, dependencies, or external assumptions.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Scenario comparison
requirement: The platform shall compare at least three scenarios side by side using mission outcomes, projects delivered, delays, resource gaps, cost, risk, benefits, and impacted stakeholders.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Priority-change impact
requirement: Changing the priority of a demand or project shall show resulting effects on resource contention, core-function coverage, schedules, costs, and lower-priority work.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Budget-change impact
requirement: A proposed budget increase, reduction, delay, or funding restriction shall show affected scope, milestones, staffing, contracts, benefits, and mission outcomes.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Workforce-change impact
requirement: A proposed gain, loss, vacancy, detail, reassignment, or contractor change shall show impacts to skill coverage, capacity, schedules, risk, and mission functions.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Schedule-change impact
requirement: A milestone or dependency date change shall propagate through linked tasks and portfolio dependencies and identify decisions or deliverables placed at risk.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Demand-surge analysis
requirement: Leaders shall model emergent demand surges and identify work that must stop, slip, reduce, or receive additional resources to preserve priority outcomes.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Core-function protection
requirement: Scenarios shall enforce or visibly flag minimum coverage constraints for designated core functions and critical skills.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Confidence and uncertainty
requirement: Forecasts shall show the confidence, source, date, owner, and range or sensitivity of material assumptions rather than presenting uncertain estimates as precise facts.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Scenario recommendation
requirement: The platform shall allow analysts to document a recommended scenario, tradeoffs, rejected alternatives, conditions, and the decision requested from leadership.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Scenario approval and promotion
requirement: An approved scenario shall be promotable to a new baseline through controlled change, preserving the prior baseline and decision record.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Forecast accuracy
requirement: The platform shall compare prior forecasts to actual results for schedule, effort, cost, demand, and benefits and report forecast bias and accuracy over time.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Early warning indicators
requirement: The platform shall surface leading indicators such as rising unplanned work, milestone confidence decline, resource churn, unfilled critical roles, stale status, and repeated financial variance.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
title: Decision latency impact
requirement: The platform shall estimate and report the effect of overdue approvals or unresolved decisions on schedule, cost, resource use, and mission outcomes.
capability: Forecasting, Scenario Planning, and Change Impact
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Senior dashboard
requirement: The DDC5I senior-level dashboard shall provide enterprise priorities, mission alignment, demand versus capacity, portfolio health, resource and skill gaps, budget and actuals, roadblocks, dependencies, decisions required, and forecast change impacts.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Division dashboard
requirement: Each division dashboard shall show its missions and core functions, demand pipeline, approved projects, schedule and financial health, workforce capacity, skill gaps, roadblocks, dependencies, benefits, and required leader actions.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: User project dashboard
requirement: Each user shall have a personalized view of assigned projects, tasks, approvals, actions, risks, time or effort updates, upcoming milestones, and notifications.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Drill-down traceability
requirement: Every aggregate metric shall drill down to the contributing divisions, projects, demands, resources, financial records, assumptions, and data freshness needed to explain it.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Priority and gap view
requirement: Senior leaders shall see approved priority order, priority conflicts, mission gaps, capacity gaps, skill gaps, funding gaps, and work at risk.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Planned versus actual view
requirement: Dashboards shall compare planned and actual schedule, effort, staffing, cost, deliverables, and benefits with clear variance thresholds and explanations.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Roadblock and decision view
requirement: Dashboards shall rank unresolved roadblocks and decisions by mission impact, urgency, affected projects, aging, and accountable owner.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Dependency view
requirement: Leaders shall visualize critical internal and external dependencies, lead and support organizations, required contributions, and downstream impacts.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Resource heatmaps
requirement: Dashboards shall display capacity, allocation, over-allocation, vacancies, skill shortages, and core-function coverage by division, team, role, skill, and time period.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Financial dashboard
requirement: Authorized leaders shall view budget, actuals, forecast, variance, burn rate, estimate at completion, unfunded requirements, and expiring or at-risk funding.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Forecast dashboard
requirement: Leaders shall compare the approved baseline with proposed scenarios and see mission, project, resource, cost, risk, and stakeholder impacts.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: DDC5I story
requirement: The platform shall generate an audience-tailored narrative connecting DDC5I mission, division functions, key projects, resources applied, outcomes, and value to the Joint Staff and Joint or coalition forces.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Purpose-end state-value card
requirement: Every core function and named project shall present a concise card containing purpose, problem, end state, sponsor, lead and support relationships, resources, key outcomes, ROI or value, and consequences of inaction.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Executive brief generation
requirement: Authorized users shall generate a current executive brief in approved Word, PowerPoint, PDF, or print-ready formats with selectable division, time period, audience, and classification markings.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Recurring report packs
requirement: The platform shall schedule, generate, and distribute weekly, monthly, quarterly, and ad hoc portfolio reports to authorized stakeholder groups using governed definitions and data cutoffs.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Metric definitions
requirement: Each dashboard metric shall expose its definition, formula, source, owner, refresh time, thresholds, and known limitations.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Role and data filters
requirement: Dashboards shall support authorized filtering by division, mission, function, project, priority, status, sponsor, customer, resource, skill, fiscal year, and scenario.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Saved views and subscriptions
requirement: Users shall save permitted filters, create personal or shared views, and subscribe to scheduled snapshots or exception-based notifications.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Accessibility and export
requirement: Dashboards and reports shall meet applicable Section 508 requirements and export accessible, readable data without removing security markings or access controls.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

domain: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
title: Health-state standardization and provenance
requirement: Dashboards shall use governed health states such as on track, at risk, off track, blocked, and completed and shall distinguish system-calculated health, owner-reported health, and authorized overrides.
capability: Dashboards, Reporting, and the DDC5I Story
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: In-platform collaboration
requirement: Authorized users shall comment, mention colleagues, ask questions, attach evidence, and resolve discussion threads on demands, projects, risks, decisions, resources, and costs.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Notification rules
requirement: Users and administrators shall configure event, threshold, digest, reminder, and escalation notifications while preventing unnecessary duplicate messages.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Outlook notifications
requirement: The platform shall send Outlook-compatible notifications for submissions, assignments, approvals, due dates, exceptions, status changes, and escalations with a secure link to the authoritative record.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Outlook actionable approval
requirement: Where approved by the Microsoft 365 tenant and security authority, recipients shall be able to approve, reject, request changes, or open the record from an Outlook actionable message, with server-side validation at action time.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Stale actionable message protection
requirement: An Outlook approval message shall not permit action after the item is withdrawn, changed materially, delegated, expired, or decided; the current status shall be displayed or retrieved before acceptance.

title: Calendar integration

title: Authorization boundary
requirement: The solution shall be deployed only within an environment whose authorization boundary and impact level are approved for the information it will process.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: CAC or PIV authentication
requirement: The platform shall support the approved enterprise single sign-on mechanism, including CAC or PIV authentication where required, and shall not create unmanaged local accounts except controlled break-glass accounts.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Least-privilege access
requirement: Access shall be role-based and, where required, attribute-based using least privilege, separation of duties, division scope, project membership, sensitivity, and need to know.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Row and field security
requirement: The platform shall enforce record- and field-level access for sensitive demands, personnel attributes, cost rates, acquisition data, legal matters, and other restricted information.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: External user isolation
requirement: If external stakeholders are granted access, they shall be restricted to explicitly shared records and fields and shall not infer enterprise portfolio information through search, counts, exports, notifications, or APIs.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Data marking
requirement: The platform shall display and preserve applicable classification, CUI, dissemination, privacy, and records markings on screens, exports, reports, attachments, notifications, and API responses.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Encryption
requirement: The solution shall use approved cryptography for data in transit and at rest, including backups, integration payloads, and file storage.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Audit logging
requirement: The platform shall log authentication, access to sensitive records, creates, updates, deletes, exports, imports, approvals, delegations, configuration changes, integration activity, and administrative actions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Tamper-evident audit
requirement: Audit records shall be protected from unauthorized modification and retained, searchable, exportable, and time-synchronized according to policy.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Separation of duties
requirement: The platform shall support separation among requester, assessor, approver, financial certifier, resource approver, administrator, auditor, and data steward roles and shall report conflicting assignments.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Session security
requirement: The solution shall enforce approved session timeout, reauthentication for sensitive actions, concurrent session, device, and failed-login controls.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Malware and file control
requirement: Uploaded files shall be type- and size-validated, scanned using approved protections, and prevented from executing active content not permitted by policy.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Records management
requirement: Records shall be categorized, retained, frozen under legal or records hold, transferred, and disposed according to approved schedules, with disposition auditable.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Privacy controls
requirement: The platform shall support privacy minimization, purpose limitation, access accounting, appropriate notices, and handling rules for personally identifiable information.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Backup and recovery security
requirement: Backups and recovery environments shall inherit access, encryption, marking, retention, and audit protections equivalent to production.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Secure API
requirement: APIs shall use approved authentication and authorization, rate limits, input validation, logging, secrets management, and protection against replay and mass extraction.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Security testing
requirement: The solution shall complete required architecture review, threat modeling, vulnerability scanning, code or configuration review, penetration testing, and remediation before production release and after material change.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Control inheritance and evidence
requirement: The implementation shall document applicable inherited and system-specific controls and retain evidence needed for risk management and continuous monitoring.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Secure notification content
requirement: Email and calendar notifications shall contain only the minimum approved information and shall direct users to the authenticated platform for sensitive details.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Availability
requirement: The production service shall meet an approved availability target and maintenance window, with initial objective of at least 99.9 percent monthly availability excluding approved maintenance.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Interactive performance
requirement: For normal load, 95 percent of common page views and record actions shall complete within three seconds, excluding external integration latency and documented long-running analytics.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Dashboard performance
requirement: Division and senior dashboards shall render current cached results within three seconds for the 95th percentile under the approved concurrent-user load.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Scalability
requirement: The platform shall scale to enterprise growth in users, divisions, portfolio items, tasks, attachments, financial records, forecasts, and integrations without redesign of the core data model.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Responsive design
requirement: Core functions shall be usable on approved desktop, laptop, and tablet screen sizes without horizontal scrolling for primary workflows.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Accessibility
requirement: The user interface, generated reports, and user documentation shall conform to applicable Section 508 requirements and WCAG 2.2 Level AA, verified through automated and manual testing.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Usability
requirement: Representative users shall complete defined intake, assessment, approval, project update, resource assignment, and executive drill-down tasks with target success rates established during usability testing.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Browser support
requirement: The solution shall support the enterprise-approved versions of Microsoft Edge and other designated browsers, with a published compatibility and deprecation policy.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Reliability
requirement: Business transactions shall be atomic or recoverable, and retries shall not create duplicate demands, approvals, projects, assignments, actuals, or notifications.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Recovery objectives
requirement: The service shall meet approved recovery time and recovery point objectives established by mission impact analysis and verified through periodic recovery exercises.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Observability
requirement: Operations staff shall have metrics, logs, traces, health checks, alerting, and runbooks sufficient to detect and diagnose service, workflow, data, and integration failures.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Maintainability
requirement: Configuration, integrations, calculations, templates, and extensions shall be documented, version controlled, testable, and designed to minimize unsupported customization.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Upgradeability
requirement: The solution shall preserve required functionality through vendor upgrades using automated regression testing and a documented review of deprecated features and custom extensions.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Interoperability
requirement: Interfaces shall use approved open or well-documented standards and shall avoid unnecessary proprietary coupling between the portfolio platform and ProjectOS.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Data portability
requirement: Authorized administrators shall export complete business records, relationships, attachments metadata, history, and configuration in documented machine-readable formats for continuity and transition.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Localization and time
requirement: The platform shall store timestamps consistently, display the user's authorized time zone, support fiscal calendars, and avoid ambiguity in date formats and deadline calculations.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Supportability
requirement: The operating model shall define service desk tiers, incident and problem processes, request fulfillment, knowledge articles, maintenance communications, and service-level reporting.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Low-code configuration
requirement: Authorized administrators shall configure forms, fields, reference values, workflows, scoring models, thresholds, dashboards, and notifications using governed low-code or no-code capabilities where practical.

requirement: Configuration changes shall move through development, test, and production environments using documented release, testing, approval, and rollback controls.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Reference-data stewardship
requirement: Named data stewards shall manage controlled vocabularies, hierarchies, aliases, effective dates, and retirement of reference values.

title: Required-field quality
requirement: The platform shall enforce completeness, format, range, relationship, and business-rule validation at entry, import, integration, and approval points.

requirement: Data stewards shall view completeness, validity, uniqueness, consistency, timeliness, reconciliation, and orphan-record measures by division and source.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Stale-data control
requirement: The platform shall flag stale project status, resource plans, costs, forecasts, assumptions, benefits, risks, and stakeholder commitments based on configurable currency rules.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Duplicate and merge
requirement: Authorized stewards shall identify, review, merge, and unmerge duplicate records while preserving identifiers, relationships, history, and source lineage.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Bulk administration
requirement: Authorized administrators shall perform controlled bulk reassignment, status update, reference update, archive, and correction with preview, approval where required, and audit.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

requirement: The platform shall support controlled activation of new modules, integrations, and workflow changes by environment, division, or pilot group.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Configuration inventory
requirement: The service shall maintain an inventory of custom fields, workflows, scripts, integrations, reports, roles, and extensions with owner, purpose, version, and upgrade impact.

title: Usage analytics
requirement: Administrators shall monitor adoption, active users, workflow completion, abandoned intake, dashboard use, notification volume, and help demand without exposing unnecessary personal monitoring data.

requirement: The service shall provide non-production environments and sanitized training data for configuration testing, user training, and integration validation.

requirement: Authorized administrators shall configure approved business-object types, fields, relationships, statuses, validation rules, and lifecycle behavior through governed configuration without unsupported source-code changes.

title: Optional decision support
requirement: AI-enabled functions shall be optional decision support and shall not autonomously approve, reject, fund, cancel, or reassign mission work or resources.

title: Summarization
requirement: Authorized users may generate draft summaries of demands, project status, risks, decisions, and portfolio changes, with source links and mandatory human review before publication.

title: Recommendation transparency
requirement: AI recommendations shall identify the data used, material assumptions, confidence or limitations, and the rule or model version sufficient for an authorized reviewer to assess the recommendation.

title: No fabricated facts
requirement: Generated portfolio narratives shall distinguish sourced facts, calculated values, analyst judgments, and generated suggestions and shall not insert unsupported mission, cost, resource, or performance claims.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Sensitive-data boundary
requirement: AI processing shall occur only within approved environments and data boundaries, and prompts, outputs, embeddings, and logs shall inherit applicable access and retention controls.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

title: Bias and quality evaluation
requirement: AI models used for prioritization support, duplicate detection, forecasting, or recommendations shall be tested for accuracy, bias, drift, explainability, and mission-relevant failure modes.

title: Human feedback
requirement: Users shall be able to accept, modify, or reject AI suggestions and provide structured feedback without overwriting the authoritative source data.

title: AI audit
requirement: The platform shall log material AI-assisted outputs, model or service version, user, source records, disposition, and final human-approved content as required by policy.

requirement: Each release shall trace approved requirements to design, configuration or code, test cases, defects, user acceptance, training, and production verification.

requirement: The product team shall maintain a governed backlog, quarterly value review, user feedback process, performance analytics, and roadmap for iterative improvement.
acceptance_notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.
